decisions.title: Performance concerns identified for Horizon Dashboard
decisions.detail: There are concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance.

decisions.title: Consideration of a better database for the project
decisions.detail: A suggestion was made to potentially use a better database for the project at some point in the future. This is not a firm decision but a topic for consideration.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI aesthetic improvement required
decisions.detail: The User Interface of the dashboard project needs to be improved visually to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Agreement on inadequacy of current UI design
decisions.detail: All attendees agreed that the current design of the UI is not satisfactory.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Timelines discussed but no firm decisions made
decisions.detail: A discussion regarding project timelines occurred, but no concrete decisions or agreements were reached.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Uncertainty regarding CRM system integration
decisions.detail: There is a potential future need to integrate with the CRM system, but the specific timing and method of this integration are currently unknown and require further clarification.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually, but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget identified as a concern
decisions.detail: Budget was briefly mentioned and is a concern, though no specific financial figures were disclosed.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Recommendation for more thorough testing
decisions.detail: The team lead suggested that testing procedures should be more comprehensive going forward.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Need for improved documentation
decisions.detail: There is a perceived need to improve documentation practices for future work.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Redesign and implement User Interface for Horizon Dashboard to meet aesthetic improvement requirements (D003, D004)
Implement more comprehensive testing procedures and/or frameworks (D008, inferred_needs_confirmation)
Improve documentation practices for current and future engineering work (D009, inferred_needs_confirmation)

constraints.text: Address identified performance concerns for the Horizon Dashboard (D001).
Budget is a concern, requiring cost-conscious technical decisions (D007).

open_technical_questions: Evaluation and selection of a 'better database' for the project; confirmation needed on this suggested architectural change (D002, inferred_needs_confirmation).
What are the specific technical requirements, integration points, and timeline for potential CRM system integration? (D006)

acceptance_criteria: Given a new UI design has been implemented for the Horizon Dashboard, When the UI is visually inspected, Then it must conform to all specified aesthetic and usability standards (e.g., mockups, style guides, color palettes, typography standards).
Given a new UI design has been implemented, When the UI is interacted with, Then it must be responsive and adapt correctly across a range of common screen sizes and device types.
test_scenarios: Verify that all dashboard components (e.g., charts, tables, navigation, buttons) reflect the new visual design consistently across the application.
Confirm that text readability is improved and fonts are aesthetically pleasing according to the new design.
Test the responsiveness of the dashboard by resizing the browser window to various breakpoints to ensure elements scale and rearrange correctly.
Validate that interactive elements (buttons, links, form fields) have clear visual states (e.g., hover, active, disabled) that align with the new design.
unresolved_behaviors: Define specific, measurable performance metrics and targets for the Horizon Dashboard (e.g., maximum load times for key sections, response times for critical user actions, concurrent user capacity) to address identified concerns (D001).

Provide detailed design specifications, mockups, or a comprehensive style guide that objectively defines what 'nicer' UI aesthetic entails, including visual standards, interaction patterns, and usability guidelines (D003, D004).
Clarify the scope, specific timing, and technical method of the potential CRM system integration, including data synchronization requirements and user interaction points (D006).

decision_history: It was decided that the User Interface of the Horizon Dashboard project needs significant visual improvement (D003).
All attendees agreed that the current design of the UI is unsatisfactory, which reinforces the decision for aesthetic improvement (D004).
dependencies: Potential future integration with the CRM system is a dependency, but the specific timing and method are currently unknown (D006).
risks: Performance concerns have been identified for the Horizon Dashboard project, which could impact user satisfaction and project viability (D001).
Budget has been identified as a concern; without specific financial figures, this poses a risk to project resources and scope stability (D007). Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High.
The unconfirmed suggestion for a 'better database' (D002) poses a risk of scope creep or significant technical rework if adopted later in the project lifecycle. Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High.
The recommendation for more thorough testing (D008) is unconfirmed, risking quality issues if current testing procedures remain inadequate. Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium.

The perceived need for improved documentation (D009) is unconfirmed, risking poor knowledge transfer and increased overhead for future maintenance if not addressed. Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium.
alignment_gaps: Project timelines were discussed, but no firm decisions or agreements were reached, indicating a lack of clear commitment or understanding among stakeholders on delivery schedule (D005).
There's an unconfirmed suggestion to consider a 'better database' (D002), which might indicate an unaligned technical direction or an unconfirmed scope item.
open_questions: What are the confirmed project timelines and key delivery milestones for the Horizon Dashboard project? (PM owned: Establish project schedule) (D005)

Is the suggestion to use a 'better database' a confirmed requirement, and if so, what is the scope and impact of this change on the current project plan? (PM owned: Scope clarification and confirmation) (D002)
What are the specific requirements for 'more thorough testing', and how will they be implemented and validated? (PM/QA owned: Requirements definition) (D008)
What are the specific needs for 'improved documentation practices', and what standards should be adopted for future work? (PM owned: Requirements definition) (D009)
What is the confirmed timing and preferred method for integrating with the CRM system? (PM owned: Dependency management and scope definition) (D006)

system_requirements: The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL provide improved performance to address identified concerns. (derived from D001)
The Horizon Dashboard system's User Interface (UI) SHALL be aesthetically improved to enhance visual appeal and user experience. (D003, D004)
The Horizon Dashboard system's quality SHALL be ensured through comprehensive testing procedures. (inferred from D008 - needs confirmation of specific testing requirements)
data_flows: Data MAY flow between the Horizon Dashboard and an external CRM system in the future. (inferred from D006)

constraints.text: The existing Horizon Dashboard system currently exhibits performance concerns. (D001)
The project budget is a concern and may impose limitations on technical solutions or resources. (D007)

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific functional and non-functional requirements that necessitate the use of a 'better' database (e.g., scalability, data volume, query complexity), and what alternative database technologies are being evaluated for the Horizon Dashboard? (Owned by SA for D002)

What data entities, attributes, and relationships are required for the potential CRM system integration, what are the triggers for data exchange, and what integration patterns (e.g., API, messaging, batch) are preferred or mandated? (Owned by SA for D006)

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has identified critical performance issues and a need for significant visual improvements to the Horizon Dashboard, which could affect user experience and adoption. We also have open questions regarding project timelines, budget, and future integration with our CRM system. Addressing these will be key to the project's success and user satisfaction.
key_impact: Slow performance could frustrate users and limit the dashboard's effectiveness for key business operations.
An outdated user interface will negatively impact user adoption and reflect poorly on the product's overall quality.
Unclear timelines and budget could lead to project delays or unforeseen financial challenges.
Unresolved questions about integrating with our customer relationship management (CRM) system could hinder data flow and efficiency for sales and customer service teams.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirming the exploration and potential adoption of a better database system for enhanced project stability and scalability.
Approving the recommendation for more comprehensive testing procedures to ensure a higher quality product for users.
Agreeing on the need to improve documentation practices for better project understanding and future maintainability.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the definitive timelines and allocated budget for completing the critical UI improvements and addressing performance concerns?
What is the strategy and timeframe for integrating the Horizon Dashboard with our CRM system?
How will the identified budget concerns be addressed to ensure the project can move forward without significant roadblocks?

issues.description: The acceptance criterion 'Given a new UI design has been implemented, When the UI is interacted with, Then it must be responsive and adapt correctly across a range of common screen sizes and device types.' is presented as a confirmed criterion. While responsiveness is a good UI quality, the source decisions (D003, D004) only state 'UI needs to look nicer' and 'current design isn't great' without explicitly mentioning responsiveness. This is an inferred need presented as a definite requirement.
issues.suggestion: Either explicitly mark this as an inferred criterion requiring confirmation or rephrase it to reflect it as a best practice or a derived requirement rather than a direct extraction from the source decisions.

review.reviewer_notes: Overall, the reports are well-structured and traceable to the extracted decisions. There is one warning regarding an inferred UI responsiveness requirement being stated as a firm acceptance criterion in the QA view without explicit source evidence. No critical hallucinations or missing sources were found. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.
